content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 18, 1987

Susan M. Schechtman
City Attorney
City of Pacifica
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, CA 94044

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-031

Dear Ms. Schechtman:

You have written with the authorization of Pacifica Planning
Commissioner Larry Kasparowitz requesting advice regarding his
duties under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Because of
a decision pending on February 17, I provided you with telephone
advice on February 11. This letter confirms that advice.

QUESTION

May Mr. Kasparowitz participate in decisions affecting a
developer's project if the developer previously employed an
architect who subcontracted landscape architectural work to
Mr. Kasparowitz' firm?

CONCLUSION

Based upon the specific facts provided, Mr. Kasparowitz is
not required to disqualify himself from participating in
decisions affecting the subject project.

FACTS

Mr. Kasparowitz is a one-third partner in the firm of
Austern/Kasparowitz. The firm provides architectural, landscape
architectural and urban design services. Within the past 12
months, the firm has received more than $2,500 for services
rendered to Gerald Laub, another architect. Mr. Laub selected
the firm to do the work and he subcontracted landscape

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Susan M. Schechtman
February 18, 1987
Page 2


architectural work to the firm. The work involved was for a
project in Pacifica which Mr. Laub was working on as architect
for developer Ron Sette.

Mr. Sette has a new project (Mori Point) which will be
coming before the Pacifica Planning Commission for review.2/
Mr. Laub will not be receiving any work from Mr. Sette for any
portion of the Mori Point project. Another architect has
already been chosen. Neither Mr. Kasparowitz nor his firm will
receive any work from Mr. Sette for any portion of the Mori
Point project. The consultants have already been chosen.
Furthermore, Mr. Kasparowitz does not anticipate that he or his
firm will receive any work from the ultimate homeowners at the
Mori Point project.

It is possible that Mr. Kasparowitz or his firm may do
other work in the future for Gerald Laub in connection with
other projects.3/ It is also possible that Mr. Kasparowitz'
firm might do work for Ron Sette in the future on other
projects, but only as subcontractor consultants to Gerald Laub.

ANALYSIS

The Act provides that no public official shall make,
participate in making or use his or her official position to
influence the making of a governmental decision in which he or
she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)

An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment
worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more....


2/ The Mori Point project includes 60 single-family
residences as well as a 200-room conference center hotel.

3/ Mr. Kasparowitz' statement of economic interests for
1985 indicates that he also did work for Mr. Laub in that year.

Susan M. Schechtman
February 18, 1987
Page 3


        (c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

                              Section 87103.

Mr. Kasparowitz is a public official. In his role as
planning commissioner, he makes or participates in making a
governmental decision if he becomes involved in decisions
regarding the Mori Point project.

Mr. Kasparowitz has an investment in his firm which is
presumably worth $1,000 or more. (Section 82034.) As a
one-third owner of the firm, one-third of the gross receipts of
the firm are attributable to him. The firm's clients are the
sources of this income. (Sections 82030(a), 87207.)
Consequently, Gerald Laub is a source of income to
Mr. Kasparowitz of more than $800 during the preceding 12
months. Under the facts presented, the developer, Ron Sette, is
not considered a source of income to Mr. Kasparowitz' firm since
the decision to subcontract to the firm was made entirely by
Mr. Laub without any input from Mr. Sette.4/

Mr. Kasparowitz must disqualify himself from decisions which
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect
upon his firm or upon Mr. Laub if the effects are distinguish-
able from the effects upon the public generally. (Sections
87100, 87103.) The facts provided by you state that the
decisions on the Mori Point project will have no reasonably
foreseeable effect upon either Mr. Laub or upon Mr. Kasparowitz'
firm. Consequently, no disqualification should be required.

_____________________________
4/ See enclosed copies of advice letters to Gordon M. Hart,
A-81-264 (Jan. 6, 1984); J. Steven Lempel, No. A-85-193
(Sept. 19, 1985); and Thomas J. Harron, No. A-84-288 (Dec. 21,
1984).

Susan M. Schechtman
February 18, 1987
Page 4


·    Should you have any questions regarding this letter, I may
be reached at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible signature]
                                        By:  Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:REL:plh:

CITY HALL • 170 SANTA MARIA AVENUE • PACIFICA, CALIFORNIA 94044

MAYOR
Jon Galehouse

MAYOR PRO TEM
Ginny Silva Jaquith

COUNCIL
Chards DH Curry
Peter Lyda
Fred Howard

Scenic Pacifica

January 20, 1987

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice Letter - REQUEST RESPONSE BY FEBRUARY 17, 1987

Dear Sir or Madam:

Please provide me with a written Advice Letter regarding a potential conflict
of interest question concerning a City of Pacifica Planning Commissioner, Larry
Kasparowitz. Mr. Kasparowitz has authorized me to week written advice from your
agency. The following is a summary of the facts and the question presented.

FACTS

Mr. Kasparowitz is a Planning Commissioner in the City of Pacifica. He is
a professional architect and landscape architect. He is engaged in private
practice in the City of San Francisco.

In 1986, Mr. Kasparowitz was hired by a Pacifica architect, Gerald Laub, to
do landscape architecture work for a project. The project developer was Mr. Ron
Sette. Mr. Laub selected Mr. Kasparowitz' firm to do the work. The developer
did not solicit nor encourage the selection of Mr. Kasparowitz. The contract
was signed between Gerald Laub and Mr. Kasparowitz. A copy of the contract is
attached hereto.

Mr. Kasparowitz' firm completed work on this contract in 1986 and was paid
a total of $2,500.00 for the work.

Mr. Sette has a new project which will come before the Pacifica Planning
Commission in February and March of 1987. Mr. Laub will not be receiving any
work from Mr. Sette for any portion of the Mori Point project. For will Mr.
Kasparowitz be receiving work from Mr. Sette for any portion of the project.
It is not foreseeable that the Mori Point project will generate work for Mr.
Kasparowitz. However, it is possible that Mr. Kasparowitz or his firm may do
other work in the future for Gerald Laub in connection with other projects.
It is also possible that Mr. Kasparowitz might do work for Ron Sette in the

Path of Portola 1769 - San Francisco Bay Discovery Site

Fair Political Practices Commission
January 20, 1987
Page Two


future on other projects, but only as subcontractors to Gerald Laub. (The attached
letter from Mr. Kasparowitz sets out in greater detail the facts of this situation.)

Mr. Sette's new project will come before the Planning Commission for a hearing
on the EIR on February 17, 1987. For that reason, it would be greatly appreciated
if you could provide me with a letter opinion prior to that date so that Mr.
Kasparowitz can be advised of any potential conflict of interests. Please call
me if you need any additional information or facts concerning this matter and I
will provide every assistance.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
SUSAN M. SCHECTMAN
City Attorney

SMS:jh

Enclosures

cc: Larry Kasparowitz - w/o enclosures

AUSTERN
KASPAROWITZ
& CHUNG

Architecture and Planning
Landscape Architecture
Urban Design


Susan M. Schectman, City Attorney
City of Pacifica
City Hall
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, CA, 94044


December 30, 1986


RE: Potential Conflict of Interest


Dear Ms. Schectman,

Please provide me with a written opinion regarding my potential conflict of interest regarding the upcoming project on Mori Point. I will summarize the relevant facts to the best of my ability;

1. I am currently a Planning Commissioner in the City of Pacifica and was appointed in February 1985.

2. I am licensed as both an Architect and Landscape Architect in the State of California.


1095 Market Street, Suite 403
San Francisco, California 94103
(415) 863-2570

3. I was approached by Gerald Laub, Architect to do the landscape and irrigation work for a garden apartment project on Lewis Lane and Norfolk Drive in Pacifica. The project developer was Ron Sette. Gerry told me that he had mentioned to Ron that my firm could do the work. The contract was signed between Gerald Laub and my firm (by myself) on February 17, 1986 (a copy is included).

4. I declared a conflict of interest both times when the project came to the Planning Commission.

5. The work was completed on June 4, 1986 and our firm received its last payment from Mr. Laub on August 18, 1986.

6. Mr. Laub paid our firm $2500.00 for the planting and irrigation plans. We subcontracted the irrigation to a consultant, Martin Blackman of San Francisco.

7. I was also asked by Mr. Laub to do the layout plans for the project (not included in our original scope of services), and we did that for an additional $281.25.

8. Mr. Sette has developed a new project which will come before the Planning Commission in February and March of 1987, called the Mori Point Project. This project includes sixty single-family residences as well as a two hundred room conference center hotel.

9. Mr. Laub will not be receiving work from Mr. Sette for any portion of the Mori Point Project (the architect has already been chosen).

10. Neither I not my firm will be receiving work from Mr. Sette for any portion of the Mori Point Project (the consultants have already been chosen).

11. It is possible that I or my firm may do other work for Gerald Laub in the future.

12. I cannot guess whether I or my firm may do work for Ron Sette in the future, but we would be consultants to Gerald Laub were that to happen.

13. I do not anticipate receiving work from the homeowners at Mori Point.

14. Neither I nor my firm have any type of formal relationship with either Gerald Laub or Ron Sette.

Please contact me if you have any further questions or if I can be of help in determining my position regarding the potential conflict of interest in the discussions and vote on the Mori Point Project.

Sincerely,

[illegible]

Lawrence Kasparowitz
Architect & Landscape Architect


CC: Wendy Cosin, Planning Administrator

Gerald Laub, Architect
263 Kent Road
Pacifica, CA
94044


February 16, 1986


Dear Gerry,

This letter outlines the scope of services for landscaping and
irrigation design of twelve lots containing forty two units at
Lewis Lane and Norfolkt Lane in Pacifica, California. It also
sets a price for those services.

After initial discussions with the architect and the developer,
we shall prepare the following documents:

     a. Planting Plans -- showing at a scale of 1" = 20'; the
arrangement and identification of ground covers, shrubs and
trees, including a plant list with common and latin names, sizes
and spacing for all plant materials.

     b. Planting Details and Specifications -- showing at
appropriate scales: staking details for trees, planting details
for ground covers, and miscellaneous other details as required.
Specifications and general notes shall be included on the working
drawings.

     c. Irrigation Plans -- showing at a scale of 1" = 20';
arrangement and identification of irrigation piping, sprinkler
heads, controller valves and backflow preventers designed in a
manner which will adequately water all plant materials in the
flatter portions of the site and the areas immediately adjacent
to the uphill units.

     d. Irrigation Details and Specifications -- showing at
appropriate scales: details for sprinkler installation,
backflow preventers, and other installation requirements as
necessary. Specifications and general notes shall be included on
the working drawings.




Austern • Kasparowitz
Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Urban Design

4083 NINETEENTH STREET • SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94114 • (415) 626-3313

The price for the above described services is a fixed fee of
*2500.00, and is based on the site plans as submitted to the
City of Pacifica Planning Commission, the topographic map as
supplied, and is based on the following exclusions:

1. The civil engineer will be responsible for all grading, both
rough and finish.
2. The architect or civil engineer will be responsible for all
retaining walls, paving, and hardscape.

Balances will be billed monthly. Upon return of this proposal
with your signature and a deposit of *250.00, we will be pleased
to proceed with the project.


Gerald Laub                                                    4/17/86
Gerald Laub, Architect                                         date


[illegible]                                                    4/17/86
for Austern/Kasparowitz                                        date




Austern · Kasparowitz
Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Urban Design

4083 NINETEENTH STREET · SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94114 · (415) 626-3313

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 26, 1987




Susan M. Schectman
City Attorney
City Hall
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, CA  94044

Re:  87-031

Dear Ms. Schectman:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 21, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert E. Leidigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days.  You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

                              Very truly yours,


                              [signature]
                              Diane M. Griffiths
                              General Counsel

The Act provides that no public official shall make, participate in making or use his or her official position to influence the making of a governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

2/ The Mori Point project includes 60 single-family residences as well as a 200-room conference center hotel.

3/ Mr. Kasparowitz' statement of economic interests for 1985 indicates that he also did work for Mr. Laub in that year.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

Section 87103.

Mr. Kasparowitz is a public official. In his role as planning commissioner, he makes or participates in making a governmental decision if he becomes involved in decisions regarding the Mori Point project.

Mr. Kasparowitz has an investment in his firm which is presumably worth $1,000 or more. (Section 82034.) As a one-third owner of the firm, one-third of the gross receipts of the firm are attributable to him. The firm's clients are the sources of this income. (Sections 82030(a), 87207.) Consequently, Gerald Laub is a source of income to Mr. Kasparowitz of more than $800 during the preceding 12 months. (Sections 87100, 87103.) The facts provided by you state that the decisions on the Mori Point project will have no reasonably foreseeable effect upon either Mr. Laub or upon Mr. Kasparowitz' firm. 4/ See enclosed copies of advice letters to Gordon M. Hart, A-83-264 (Jan. 6, 1984); J. Steven Lempel, No. A-85-193 (Sept. 19, 1985); and Thomas J. Harron, No. A-84-288 (Dec. 21, 1984).

QUESTION: May Mr. Kasparowitz participate in decisions affecting a developer's project if the developer previously employed an architect who subcontracted landscape architectural work to Mr. Kasparowitz' firm?

CONCLUSION: Based upon the specific facts provided, Mr. Kasparowitz is not required to disqualify himself from participating in decisions affecting the subject project.
